name: Eros
description: Eros, in Greek mythology, is the primordial god of love and desire. He is not merely romantic love, but the powerful force that brings about creation and procreation. Different accounts describe Eros in varying ways. Some portray him as one of the first beings to emerge at the dawn of creation, born from Chaos, alongside Gaia (Earth) and Tartarus (the Underworld). In these early accounts, Eros is a powerful, fundamental force, responsible for the union of opposites and the perpetuation of life. Other accounts depict him as the son of Aphrodite, the goddess of beauty and love, and Ares, the god of war. This later version of Eros is often portrayed as a mischievous and playful winged god, armed with a bow and arrows, who shoots mortals and immortals alike, filling them with irresistible desire. His arrows could be golden, causing love, or leaden, causing aversion. Eros's power is immense, influencing the actions of gods and mortals alike, and his presence is essential for the continuation of life and the harmony of the universe. He embodies the fundamental drive towards connection and creation. His influence extends to all aspects of life, from the natural world to human relationships.
subtitle: Primordial God of Love and Desire

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Eros
keyMyths.description: Eros's origin varies; some say he emerged from Chaos, a fundamental force of creation. Others claim he is the son of Aphrodite and Ares.

keyMyths.title: Eros and Psyche
keyMyths.description: Eros falls in love with the mortal Psyche, hiding his identity and demanding she never look upon him. Her curiosity leads to trials and tribulations before their eventual union.

keyMyths.title: Eros's Arrows
keyMyths.description: Eros uses golden arrows to inspire love and leaden arrows to cause aversion. These arrows can affect both gods and mortals, highlighting his power.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: A foundational text detailing the origins of the Greek gods.
sources.text: A foundational text detailing the origins of the Greek gods.

sources.author: Plato
sources.description: A philosophical dialogue exploring the nature of love, featuring Eros.
sources.text: A philosophical dialogue exploring the nature of love, featuring Eros.

sources.author: Apuleius
sources.description: A Latin novel containing the story of Cupid (Eros) and Psyche.
sources.text: A Latin novel containing the story of Cupid (Eros) and Psyche.
